ddls:
CREATE TABLE "compression_methods" (
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compression method */,
  "method_name" TEXT /* Name of the compression method (e.g., LZMA, Gzip) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the compression method */,
  "algorithm" TEXT /* Algorithm used for compression (e.g., Huffman coding, LZ77) */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the compression method */,
  "implementation" TEXT /* Implementation details of the compression method */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "levels" (
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compression level */,
  "level_name" TEXT /* Name of the compression level (e.g., fastest, fast, normal) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the compression level */,
  "compression_ratio" REAL /* Expected compression ratio for the level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dictionary_sizes" (
  "size_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dictionary size */,
  "size" TEXT /* Size of the dictionary (e.g., 64 KB, 1 MB) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dictionary size */,
  "memory_requirement" INTEGER /* Memory required for the dictionary size (in MB) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("size_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system configuration */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the system configuration */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the system configuration */,
  "processor" TEXT /* Processor type (e.g., Intel Core i7, AMD Ryzen 9) */,
  "ram" INTEGER /* RAM size (in GB) */,
  "storage" INTEGER /* Storage size (in GB) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compression_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compression result */,
  "method_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compression method used */,
  "level_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compression level used */,
  "size_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dictionary size used */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system configuration used */,
  "compressing_speed" REAL /* Compressing speed (MB/s) */,
  "decompressing_speed" REAL /* Decompressing speed (MB/s) */,
  "memory_compressing" REAL /* Memory used for compressing (MB) */,
  "memory_decompressing" REAL /* Memory used for decompressing (MB) */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage percentage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compression_results_method_id FOREIGN KEY ("method_id") REFERENCES compression_methods ("method_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compression_results_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES system_configurations ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compression_results_size_id FOREIGN KEY ("size_id") REFERENCES dictionary_sizes ("size_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compression_results_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("level_id") REFERENCES levels ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compression_operations" (
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compression operation */,
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compression result */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the compression operation */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the compression operation */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the compression operation (e.g., completed, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compression_operations_result_id FOREIGN KEY ("result_id") REFERENCES compression_results ("result_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_resources" (
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system resource */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system configuration */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage percentage */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage percentage */,
  "disk_usage" REAL /* Disk usage percentage */,
  "network_usage" REAL /* Network usage percentage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_resources_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES system_configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytic */,
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compression result */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., compressing_speed, decompressing_speed) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the metric (e.g., MB/s, seconds) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_result_id FOREIGN KEY ("result_id") REFERENCES compression_results ("result_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compression result */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., compression, decompression) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_result_id FOREIGN KEY ("result_id") REFERENCES compression_results ("result_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration */,
  "config_description" TEXT /* Description of the configuration */,
  "config_type" TEXT /* Type of the configuration (e.g., system, user) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "config_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration setting */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration setting */,
  "setting_type" TEXT /* Type of the configuration setting (e.g., integer, string) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_config_settings_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
